description: Malformed MSDP payload fixtures with safe expected parser output.

origin.source: Constructed from current parser marker-skipping behavior in server/index.ts.

fixtures.description: Leading orphan markers and a missing VAL marker are skipped until a valid pair appears.
sourceFacts: server/index.ts parseMsdpPayload skips bytes until MSDP_VAR and continues when a variable is not followed by MSDP_VAL.
Phase 01 parser tests must cover malformed MSDP without crashing.
notes: The LEVEL entry is intentionally malformed because it has no VAL marker before its scalar content.

origin.source: Constructed from current parser truncated scalar behavior in server/index.ts.

fixtures.description: A variable followed by VAL and no bytes parses as an empty string rather than crashing.
sourceFacts: server/index.ts readScalar returns an empty string when called at payload end.
notes: This documents current safe parser output and does not require a behavior change in this session.

origin.source: Constructed from current parser empty-variable behavior in server/index.ts.

fixtures.description: An empty variable name is ignored while later valid pairs still parse.
sourceFacts: server/index.ts only pushes an MSDP pair when the parsed variable name is non-empty.
notes: The ignored value remains inert because its variable name is empty.

origin.source: Constructed from current parser incomplete table behavior in server/index.ts.

fixtures.description: A table without TABLE_CLOSE returns the parsed table entries safely.
sourceFacts: server/index.ts table parsing stops at payload end if TABLE_CLOSE is missing.
PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_EXITS.
notes: Future hardening may choose a stricter result, but current safe behavior is partial output.

origin.source: Constructed from current parser incomplete array behavior in server/index.ts.

fixtures.description: An array without ARRAY_CLOSE returns parsed items safely.
sourceFacts: server/index.ts array parsing stops at payload end if ARRAY_CLOSE is missing.
PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ACTIONS.
notes: Future hardening may choose a stricter result, but current safe behavior is partial output.
